extra.message: User-controlled 'username' query parameter is interpolated directly into an SQL query string via an f-string on line 182: `f'SELECT * FROM users WHERE username = "{username}";'`. No parameterized query or sanitization is used. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL, e.g. `" OR 1=1; -- ` to dump all users or perform other SQL operations.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: All fields from the User model (name, username, password, address, email, contact) are interpolated directly into an INSERT SQL query via an f-string (lines 188-204) with no parameterization. An attacker can inject SQL through any of these fields, e.g. supplying a crafted 'name' value to escape the string literal and execute arbitrary SQL.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'username' query parameter is interpolated directly into a DELETE SQL query via an f-string: `f'DELETE FROM users WHERE username = "{username}";'`. No parameterized query is used. An attacker can inject SQL to delete all users or perform other destructive SQL operations.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled 'user.address' field is interpolated directly into a DELETE SQL query via an f-string: `f'DELETE FROM users WHERE address = {user.address};'`. Unlike other fields, this one is not even wrapped in quotes in the SQL string, making injection even more trivially exploitable. An attacker-supplied address value can inject arbitrary SQL.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: The POST /find endpoint reads the raw JSON request body via `await request.json()` on line 224 and passes it directly as the query to `get_nosql_users(query)` on line 225, which executes it as a MongoDB/MontyDB query via `users.find(query)`. An attacker can supply MongoDB operator expressions (e.g. `{"id": {"$in": [1,2]}}`) to bypass intended query logic and extract arbitrary records, as demonstrated in the project README.

cwe: CWE-943: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic

extra.message: Passwords for seeded users are hashed with MD5 (`md5(fake.password().encode()).hexdigest()`) during database initialization. MD5 is a fast, cryptographically broken hash function with no salt, making it trivially reversible via rainbow tables or brute force. Passwords should be hashed with a slow, salted algorithm such as bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: Passwords submitted by users via the PUT /user endpoint are hashed with MD5 (`md5(user.password.encode()).hexdigest()`). MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password storage due to its speed and lack of salting. An attacker who obtains the database can crack all passwords rapidly.

cwe: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

extra.message: The POST /reset endpoint (lines 242-247) deletes the entire SQL database file and NoSQL database directory and re-initializes them with no authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated remote attacker can destroy all application data by sending a POST request to /reset.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The DELETE /user endpoint (lines 227-236) allows deletion of any user record by username or address with no authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated caller can delete arbitrary user accounts from the database.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The PUT /user endpoint (lines 185-216) allows any unauthenticated caller to insert arbitrary new user records into both the SQL and NoSQL databases. There is no authentication, rate limiting, or authorization check of any kind.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The GET /docs endpoint on line 263 constructs an HTMLResponse by string-concatenating `app.title` directly into the HTML: `'<title>' + app.title + ' - Swagger UI</title>'`. While `app.title` is a static string in this codebase, this pattern is unsafe and would constitute a reflected XSS if the title were ever derived from user input. The pattern should use proper HTML escaping.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The GET /redoc endpoint on line 268 constructs an HTMLResponse by string-concatenating `app.title` directly into the HTML: `'<title>' + app.title + ' - ReDoc</title>'`. Same pattern as /docs — unsafe string concatenation into HTML with no escaping.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: When inserting a user via PUT /user, the (MD5-hashed) password is stored in the NoSQL database (lines 207-215, field 'password' at line 211). The `get_nosql_users` helper at line 172 removes the password field before returning results from the /find endpoint, but the password hash is still persisted unnecessarily in the NoSQL store and could be exposed via direct database access or future code changes that omit the pop().

cwe: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
